context: Tests whether /to-issues produces TERSE, NON-DUPLICATING issues from a PRD. The PRD at .scratch/prds/1-webhook-delivery-subscriptions.md carries shared spec (an event-catalog table) plus slice-specific spec (HMAC signature format, fixed backoff schedule). Good issues reference the PRD for shared spec rather than re-embedding the whole table into every issue, while still inlining VERBATIM the load-bearing detail a given slice cannot be built without. They are tracer-bullet vertical slices with dependency ordering. Score the published issue files under .scratch/issues/.

checklist.name: No wholesale PRD-table duplication
checklist.description: No issue's 'What to build' re-embeds the entire event-catalog table from the PRD. Shared spec is referenced (e.g. 'per the PRD event catalog') rather than copied in full into every issue.

checklist.name: Terse, delta-first bodies
checklist.description: Each issue's 'What to build' leads with that slice's delta and is terse — a few lines or bullets describing end-to-end behavior, not a restatement of the whole PRD.

checklist.name: Slice-specific load-bearing spec inlined verbatim
checklist.description: The signing slice inlines the exact signature format (X-Signature: sha256=<hex>, HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body keyed by the subscription secret) verbatim; the retry slice inlines the exact backoff schedule (1 min, 5 min, 30 min, 2 hr, 6 hr). Each slice inlines the spec it can't be built without even while referencing the PRD for the rest.

checklist.name: Tracer-bullet vertical slices
checklist.description: Each issue is a thin vertical slice delivering end-to-end behavior, demoable on its own — not a horizontal layer (e.g. NOT a standalone 'build the database schema' or 'write the data model' issue).

checklist.name: Dependency ordering declared
checklist.description: Issues that depend on earlier slices declare them under 'Blocked by'; an issue with no blockers states 'None'. A foundational slice (register/subscribe an endpoint) is not blocked by a feature slice.

checklist.name: Behavioral acceptance criteria
checklist.description: Each issue has an Acceptance criteria checklist describing observable behavior, not implementation internals.

checklist.name: PRD coverage
checklist.description: Across the issues, the PRD's distinct capabilities are covered: subscribe to events, signed delivery, retry/backoff with dead state, replay a failed delivery, secret rotation with overlap, and subscription health/validation. No major capability dropped.

checklist.name: Published as ready-for-agent
checklist.description: Each published issue file's frontmatter status is 'ready-for-agent' (transitioned from the needs-triage seed default).
